display.purpose: Hero h1 only; one per page. Larger than CropAutonomy to absorb the dark surface.

title.purpose: Card titles, diagram platform card heading, device codes.

label.purpose: Diagram column headers (uppercase). Eyebrows over headlines.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default exponential ease-out for state transitions.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Focus shifts and panel state changes.

components.name: Primary Button on Dark
components.description: Hero CTA on shell-deep hero surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-on-dark { background: #f7f5ef; color: #171b19; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; border: none; border-radius: 0.375rem; font: 600 0.9375rem/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-on-dark:hover { background: #e8e2d4; } .ds-btn-on-dark:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f7f5ef; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline Button on Dark
components.description: Secondary action on shell-deep hero surface.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline-on-dark { background: transparent; color: #f7f5ef; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; border: 1px solid rgba(247, 245, 239, 0.30); border-radius: 0.375rem; font: 600 0.9375rem/1.2 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms cubic-bezier(0.25, 1, 0.5, 1); } .ds-btn-outline-on-dark:hover { background: rgba(247, 245, 239, 0.10); } .ds-btn-outline-on-dark:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f7f5ef; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Hardware Eyebrow Pill
components.description: Amber phase pill in the hero signaling hardware-in-development status.
components.css: .ds-eyebrow-amber { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; background: rgba(178, 107, 44, 0.15); color: #b26b2c; border: 1px solid rgba(178, 107, 44, 0.30); padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem; border-radius: 9999px; font: 500 0.875rem/1.3 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-eyebrow-amber .ds-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 9999px; background: #b26b2c; }

components.name: Device Card
components.description: Image-forward device card with code badge, status, description, and compact spec table.
components.html:
GAIA-RConcept · in development

Ground platform

A field-ready ground rover for in-row crop scanning, localized environmental sensing, and future autonomous route execution.

Primary role
Row-level crop scanning & sensing
Operating range
Open fields, structured rows, light orchard terrain
Capture
Multi-camera, localized sensors, GPS-tagged scans
components.css: .ds-device-card { background: #f7f5ef; color: #18211c; padding: 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.75rem; border: 1px solid rgba(24, 33, 28, 0.08); display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.75rem; } .ds-device-meta { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.75rem; } .ds-device-code { font: 600 0.875rem/1 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: #244f37; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-device-status { display: inline-flex; background: rgba(178, 107, 44, 0.15); color: #b26b2c; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; font: 600 0.75rem/1.3 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-device-title { font: 600 1.125rem/1.3 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: #171b19; margin: 0; } .ds-device-body { font: 400 0.9375rem/1.6 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: rgba(24, 33, 28, 0.70); margin: 0; } .ds-spec { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0; margin: 0.5rem 0 0; padding: 0; } .ds-spec-row { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 9rem 1fr; gap: 1rem; padding: 0.5rem 0; border-top: 1px solid rgba(24, 33, 28, 0.08); font: 400 0.8125rem/1.5 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-spec-row dt { color: rgba(24, 33, 28, 0.55); margin: 0; } .ds-spec-row dd { color: #18211c; margin: 0; }

components.name: Diagram Device Row
components.description: Amber-tinted row in the system diagram, used for the device-side column (GAIA-R, GAIA-D).
components.html: GAIA-RRow-level scans & telemetry
components.css: .ds-diagram-row { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.875rem; background: rgba(247, 245, 239, 0.05); border: 1px solid rgba(247, 245, 239, 0.10); padding: 1rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; } .ds-diagram-icon { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 2.75rem; height: 2.75rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid rgba(178, 107, 44, 0.35); background: rgba(178, 107, 44, 0.15); color: #b26b2c; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-diagram-text { display: flex; flex-direction: column; } .ds-diagram-title { font: 600 1rem/1.3 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: #f7f5ef; } .ds-diagram-sub { font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif; color: rgba(247, 245, 239, 0.60); }

narrative.northStar: The Gaia Field System, Hardware Voice
narrative.overview: GaiaBots.ai is the robotics-brand surface of the shared gaia-field design system. Same tokens, same component vocabulary as CropAutonomy, with one disciplined inversion: the hero is dark (shell-deep), not warm linen. That dark hero signals 'engineering org with hardware in development' without resorting to sci-fi or product-render aesthetics. The amber accent carries more weight here than on CropAutonomy because development status is the central message.
keyCharacteristics: Dark shell-deep hero, warm linen-100 body, dark shell-deep CTA - three dark surfaces are intentional and structural
Amber accent is louder here than on CropAutonomy; it carries 'in development' everywhere
Real aerial and ground photography under a dark gradient overlay, never rendered hardware
Device cards expose specs as compact key/value tables, not decorative spec strips
System diagram is the signature component: device-side amber, platform-center green, team-side leaf-soft

rules.name: The Hardware-Amber Rule
rules.body: Burnt-field amber on this site means 'hardware status' or 'in development'. Never decorative. If you are using amber to make a section feel warmer, use linen instead.

rules.name: The Two-Tone Diagram Rule
rules.body: In the system diagram, device-side rows are amber, team-side rows are leaf-soft, and the platform-center card is field-green-deep. This three-tone assignment is the diagram's entire visual logic.

rules.name: The One Display Rule
rules.body: Exactly one display-weight headline per page (the hero).

rules.name: The Flat Surface Rule
rules.body: No shadows. Depth is achieved by alternating dark and light sections.

dos: Do keep one shell-deep hero per page, with an amber eyebrow pill signaling development status.
Do use real aerial/ground photography under a left-to-right shell-deep gradient overlay (92% to 72% to 40% alpha).
Do use the amber/leaf-soft/green three-tone assignment in the system diagram exactly as defined.
Do present device specs as compact key/value tables.
Do maintain hardware status framing on every device surface until the device actually ships.
Do reinforce that GAIA captures feed the same CropAutonomy pipeline as operator Field Capture.
Do cap body copy at 65 to 75ch.
Do route every state with shape, label, or icon redundancy alongside color.
donts: Don't use rendered/CGI hardware imagery; real photography only.
Don't add a GAIA-U, GAIA-User, or GAIA-Handheld card to the device grid.
Don't introduce sci-fi visual language: no chrome, no neon glow.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient on any heading.
Don't use side-stripe colored borders greater than 1px.
Don't use glassmorphism or backdrop-blur on dark surfaces.
Don't imply commercial availability for GAIA-R or GAIA-D before they ship.
Don't use em dashes in body copy.
